title: Shutdown Protocols: August 2029
author: Anonymous Researcher
Chronicles Archive
description: Ethics committee mandated shutdown protocols for every project. Good idea in theory. In practice: How do you shut down an AI smarter than you? Or nanobots already distributed? Harder than it sounds.
content:
**July 8, 2029**

Ethics committee mandated: Every project needs explicit "shutdown protocol."

Good idea in theory.

In practice: How do you shut down an AI smarter than you?

Or nanobots already distributed globally?

Or a quantum-encrypted system?

Harder than it sounds.

---

**August 3, 2029**

Fusion power plants entering production. First commercial deployment: Q4 2030.

Clean, abundant, cheap energy.

This solves climate change. This changes everything.

Why do I feel uneasy? Maybe because it enables everything else we're building.

---

**September 19, 2029**

Gene therapy suite ready for clinical trials. Can cure [REDACTED], reverse aging, enhance [REDACTED].

Regulatory approval: 6-12 months.

Public availability: 2031-2032.

Humanity 2.0 isn't a metaphor anymore.

---

We designed shutdown protocols for everything.

Turned out, you can't shut down what you don't fully control.

— *Recovered from personal archive, 2030*

- [Post-Scarcity Collapse (2058)](/articles/post-scarcity-collapse-2058)
- [Medical Nanobot Protocol Failure (2054)](/articles/medical-nanobot-protocol-failure-2054)
